node_types.description: An individual receiving mental health treatment, including demographic, clinical, and psychosocial information.

properties.name: cultural_background
properties.description: Patient's cultural, religious, or spiritual background

properties.name: presenting_problems
properties.description: List of main problems or symptoms presented

properties.name: family_history
properties.description: Family mental health and developmental history

properties.name: social_history
properties.description: Social, educational, vocational, and spiritual history

properties.name: strengths
properties.description: Identified patient strengths and adaptive coping strategies

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 5 - Structure Of The First Therapy Session
Chapter 21 - Progressing As A Cognitive Behavior Therapist

node_types.description: A mental health professional providing therapy, including their training, experience, and cultural competence.

properties.name: cultural_competence
properties.description: Therapist's experience with specific cultural or religious groups

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 - Introduction To Cognitive Behavior Therapy
Chapter 21 - Progressing As A Cognitive Behavior Therapist

node_types.description: A single therapy session, including structure, agenda, and session-specific data.

properties.name: session_number
properties.description: Order of the session in the treatment sequence

properties.name: session_type
properties.description: Type of session (e.g., evaluation, standard, termination, booster)

properties.name: agenda_items
properties.description: List of agenda items for the session

properties.name: mood_check
properties.description: Mood or symptom ratings at session start

properties.name: homework_review
properties.description: Summary of homework review and completion

properties.name: problems_discussed
properties.description: Problems or topics discussed in the session

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 - Introduction To Cognitive Behavior Therapy
Chapter 5 - Structure Of The First Therapy Session
Chapter 7 - Session 2 And Beyond: Structure And Format
Chapter 8 - Problems With Structuring The Therapy Session

node_types.description: A specific clinical problem, symptom, or issue identified for intervention.

properties.name: problem_type
properties.description: Type/category of problem (e.g., depression, anxiety, avoidance)

properties.name: onset
properties.description: Onset or duration of the problem

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 5 - Structure Of The First Therapy Session

node_types.description: A treatment goal collaboratively set between patient and therapist.

properties.name: goal_type
properties.description: Type/category of goal (e.g., symptom reduction, skill acquisition)

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 5 - Structure Of The First Therapy Session

node_types.description: A structured formulation of the patient's problems, including links between thoughts, beliefs, emotions, behaviors, and history.

properties.name: intermediate_beliefs
properties.description: List of identified intermediate beliefs (rules, attitudes, assumptions)

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 13 - Identifying And Modifying Intermediate Beliefs
Chapter 14 - Identifying And Modifying Core Beliefs
Chapter 21 - Progressing As A Cognitive Behavior Therapist

node_types.description: A brief, situation-specific cognition that influences emotion and behavior.

properties.name: thought_id
properties.description: Unique identifier for the automatic thought

properties.name: situation
properties.description: Situation or trigger for the thought

properties.name: cognitive_distortion
properties.description: Type of cognitive distortion, if any

node_types.description: A rule, attitude, or assumption that mediates between core beliefs and automatic thoughts.

properties.name: belief_id
properties.description: Unique identifier for the intermediate belief

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 13 - Identifying And Modifying Intermediate Beliefs

node_types.description: A fundamental, deeply held belief about the self, others, or the world.

properties.name: core_belief_id
properties.description: Unique identifier for the core belief

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 14 - Identifying And Modifying Core Beliefs

node_types.description: An emotional state experienced by the patient, often linked to thoughts and situations.

properties.name: situation
properties.description: Situation or trigger for the emotion

node_types.description: A specific behavior or action relevant to the patient's problems or treatment.

properties.name: context
properties.description: Context or situation in which the behavior occurs

properties.name: function
properties.description: Function or purpose of the behavior (e.g., avoidance, coping)

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 15 - Additional Cognitive And Behavioral Techniques

node_types.description: A specific cognitive or behavioral intervention or technique used in therapy.

properties.name: technique_type
properties.description: Type/category (e.g., Socratic questioning, behavioral experiment, relaxation)

properties.name: cultural_adaptations
properties.description: Optional adaptations for cultural or religious alignment

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 - Introduction To Cognitive Behavior Therapy
Chapter 13 - Identifying And Modifying Intermediate Beliefs
Chapter 14 - Identifying And Modifying Core Beliefs
Chapter 15 - Additional Cognitive And Behavioral Techniques

node_types.description: A specific task or assignment given to the patient to complete between sessions.

properties.name: homework_id
properties.description: Unique identifier for the homework assignment

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 15 - Additional Cognitive And Behavioral Techniques

node_types.description: A structured plan outlining the course of therapy, including goals, interventions, and progress tracking.

properties.name: treatment_plan_id
properties.description: Unique identifier for the treatment plan

properties.name: cultural_considerations
properties.description: Cultural or religious considerations in planning

Chapter 7 - Session 2 And Beyond: Structure And Format

edge_types.description: Indicates that a therapist conducted a specific session.

edge_types.description: Indicates that a session addressed a specific clinical problem.

Chapter 7 - Session 2 And Beyond: Structure And Format

edge_types.description: Indicates that a patient has a specific clinical problem.
Indicates that a patient has a specific treatment goal.

Chapter 14 - Identifying And Modifying Core Beliefs

edge_types.description: Indicates that a cognitive conceptualization includes a specific automatic thought.
Indicates that a cognitive conceptualization includes a specific intermediate belief.

edge_types.description: Indicates that a cognitive conceptualization includes a specific core belief.

Indicates that an automatic thought leads to a specific behavior.
Indicates that an intermediate belief underlies a specific automatic thought.

edge_types.description: Indicates that a core belief underlies a specific intermediate belief.

edge_types.description: Indicates that a treatment plan targets a specific problem.
Indicates that a treatment plan includes a specific goal.
